step.title: Skill Identity
step.goal: Define the two identity fields: name and description. The description is critical for discoverability - it helps agents know when to use this skill.

substep.title: Confirm Identifier
substep.action:
The skill id was set during scaffolding: {{skill_id}}
Verify it follows conventions: - kebab-case - Starts with action verb (for workflow/exec) - Noun-based (for data) - Specific and descriptive

check.ask: Would you like to rename the skill? Current: {{skill_id}}

substep.title: Write Description
substep.ask:
Write a compelling description (max 1024 chars):
The description should answer: - What does this skill do? - When should an agent use it? - What keywords help identify relevant tasks?
Good descriptions are specific and include searchable keywords.

substep.title: Write head.md
substep.action:
Write .vera/skills/{{skill_id}}/head.md with the OpenCode skill frontmatter:
```markdown --- name: {{skill_id}} description: "{{description}}" --- ```
head.md is the FIRST include in skill.yaml (its content sits at the top of the compiled SKILL.md). OpenCode reads the --- name/description --- block to surface the skill in its picker and feed the description to invoking agents.

substep.title: Confirm Identity
substep.template-output:
Present head.md for review:
```markdown --- name: {{skill_id}} description: "{{description}}" --- ```
[a] Adjust [c] Continue [y] YOLO

section.title: name
section.description: The technical identifier used in file paths and skill invocations. This is how agents and the build system reference this skill.

rules.rule: Lowercase only, hyphens for word separation
Start with action verb for workflow/exec types

section.title: description
section.description: The human-readable explanation of what this skill does. Critical for discoverability - agents use this to decide when to invoke the skill.

rules.rule: Start with what the skill DOES (active voice)
Include keywords that match user requests

section.example: "Guides creation of VERA agent source files (agent-config.yaml) through discovery, skill architecture decisions, and section-by-section filling. Use when creating a new agent from requirements or architecture spec."
"A skill for agents." (too vague, no keywords) "Does stuff with files." (meaningless)
section.tip: Include the key nouns a user might mention: "product brief", "architecture", "requirements", "validation", etc.

example.name: Workflow Skill
example.config: name: create-product-brief description: > Guides creation of a Product Brief through collaborative discovery with the user. Covers problem statement, target users, success metrics, and competitive landscape. Use when starting a new product or feature. Produces a structured brief document ready for PRD creation.
example.rationale: Mentions: what it does (guides creation), what it produces (Product Brief), what topics it covers, when to use it, and what comes next.

example.name: Exec Skill
example.config: name: validate-yaml description: > Validates YAML files for syntax correctness and schema compliance. Returns detailed error messages with line numbers for any issues found. Use when you need to verify configuration files before deployment.
example.rationale: Clear input (YAML files), clear output (validation result with errors), clear use case (before deployment).

example.name: Data Skill
example.config: name: coding-standards description: > Reference material for code style and best practices. Covers naming conventions, file organization, error handling patterns, and testing requirements. Load when writing or reviewing code to ensure consistency.
example.rationale: Describes content (style, practices), lists topics covered, explains when to load it.
